Reservation.title: Reservation
Reservation.description: Represents a reservation at the inn.

guestName.description: Name of the guest making the reservation.

people.description: Number of people for the reservation (1-4).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the reservation was created.

params.name: reservationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the reservation.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to securely manage reservations for the inn. A top-level `reservations` collection stores all reservation documents. Since there's only a single user (owner), authorization is simplified and based on authentication. The system directly uses authentication via `request.auth.uid`, without any roles or custom claims. All reservations are placed in a flat `reservations` collection. This allows for simple queries and listing operations.

**Authorization Independence:** There is no need for authorization independence, since there is only 1 user.

**QAPs Support:** The flat `reservations` collection simplifies listing all reservations, which supports the "Ver todas as reservas" (see all reservations) feature.
